provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal
the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party

actor_claims.actor_label: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal
quote_texts: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company, its affiliates and representatives may not solicit, initiate, or knowingly encourage or facilitate alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions, or offers.
quote_texts:
Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not:

•

solicit, initiate or knowingly encourage or facilitate the making or submission of an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company, its affiliates and representatives may not participate in discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal or furnish nonpublic information in connection with an alternative proposal, except to notify the person of the non-solicitation provisions.
quote_texts: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal (except to notify such person as to the non-solicitation provisions of the merger agreement);

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company, its affiliates and representatives may not enter into an agreement relating to or providing for an alternative proposal, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
quote_texts: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal (except for confidentiality agreements permitted under the merger agreement).

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to be or reasonably likely to result in a superior proposal, the Company may provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into an acceptable confidentiality agreement.
quote_texts: if, prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s stockholders to adopt the merger agreement, the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that in the good faith judgment of the board, after consultation with outside legal and financial advisors, constitutes, or could reasonably be expected to result in, a “superior proposal”
provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to be or reasonably likely to result in a superior proposal, the Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with the third party.
quote_texts: if, prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s stockholders to adopt the merger agreement, the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that in the good faith judgment of the board, after consultation with outside legal and financial advisors, constitutes, or could reasonably be expected to result in, a “superior proposal”
engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal.

event_claims.description: Before changing its recommendation in response to a superior proposal or terminating to accept one, the Company must give Parent written notice describing the superior proposal terms.
Before changing its recommendation in response to a superior proposal or terminating to accept one, the Company must provide Parent in writing its reasons for the change or termination.
quote_texts: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Before the board may change its recommendation or the Company may terminate for a superior proposal, Parent must be given three business days to propose changes to the merger agreement, with extensions for material changes, and the Company must negotiate in good faith during that period.
quote_texts: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement (which will be extended for additional two business day periods for any material change in the superior proposal), and during this period negotiate with Parent in good faith to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: After the Parent match period and extensions, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior after considering any Parent-proposed revisions.
quote_texts: Following the end of such three business day period (and any extension thereto), after taking into account any revisions to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement proposed by Parent, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal.

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays Parent a $255 million termination fee before or substantially concurrently with termination and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for the superior proposal.
quote_texts: at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal”

long as (i) prior to or substantially concurrent with such termination, the Company has paid a termination fee of $255 million to Parent and (ii) promptly after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal;

event_claims.description: Parent may terminate the merger agreement if the board changes its recommendation before the stockholder meeting.

The Company must pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if it terminates before stockholder approval in response to a superior proposal and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for that proposal.
The Company must pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if Parent terminates the merger agreement because of a change of recommendation.
quote_texts: Parent has terminated the merger agreement in the event of a change of recommendation;
